1: Then Job answered the LORD LORD , and said,
2: And that that no thought can be withholden from thee.
3: Things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.
4: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
5: But now mine eye seeth thee.
7: And it was so so , that after the LORD LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath hath .
8: Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you after your after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is the thing which is right, like my servant Job.
9: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD LORD commanded them: the LORD LORD also accepted Job.
10: And the LORD LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
11: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.
12: So the LORD LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.
13: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
14: And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Keren-happuch.
15: And in all the land were no women found so so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
16: After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, even even four generations.
17: So Job died, being being old and full of days.
